commit.message:
Fix pyyaml/websockets iOS build failure caused by wheelhouse directory in package search (#22)

* Initial plan

* Fix pyyaml/websockets iOS build: exclude wheelhouse from package dir search

The wheelhouse/ directory was being returned by the find command before the
extracted package directory (e.g. PyYAML-6.0.3/) on macOS APFS runners due
to non-deterministic directory traversal order. Since wheelhouse/ has no
setup.py/pyproject.toml, the build validation failed.

Add -not -name "wheelhouse" to the find command in all three build job types
(build_dependency_packages, build_independent_packages, build_dependent_packages).

Co-authored-by: Creeper19472 <38857196+Creeper19472@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix token persistence and tooltip card-in-card issue

Persistence fix:
- AppDataSnapshotFactory.CreateIndexSnapshot was creating Chat

commit.message:
fix: add pathMatch full to collaborator contests redirect route

The contests/:id redirect route was using default pathMatch: 'prefix',
causing Angular to match /collaborator/contests/:id/rounds as a prefix
and redirect back to itself — a silent infinite loop that cancelled
navigation, making the Manage Contest button appear unresponsive.

Adding pathMatch: 'full' restricts the redirect to bare /contests/:id
URLs only, so /contests/:id/rounds navigates directly to the correct route.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: edvinklaebo <4660058+edvinklaebo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add singleton guard to PauseMenuBootstrap to prevent duplicate instances

PauseMenuBootstrap in MainMenu.unity has a DoNotDestoryOnLoad component on
its GameObject. Each time MainMenu reloads, a new PauseMenuBootstrap was
instantiated by the scene while the original persisted, causing Start() to
fire twice and create a second pause menu UI inside the canvas.

Added the same singleton guard pattern used by RunStatsTrackerBootstrap:
- Awake() destroys the duplicate GameObject if an instance already exists
- OnDestroy() clears the static reference so the guard resets if the real
  instance is ever destroyed

commit.message:
Update backend/orchestrator/app/store/findings_store.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: zero-frame output bug and improve code coverage

Root cause: save_fuzzed_frame used CGBitmapContextCreate with read-only
locked CVPixelBuffer data, causing NULL return (needs writable backing).
Also, fuzz() discarded all mutated frames; main() wastefully re-opened
the video 1377 times per run to save frames that never materialized.

Fixes:
- Replace CGBitmapContextCreate with CGImageCreate+CGDataProvider
  (copies pixel data, works with read-only lock)
- Save frames inline inside fuzz() instead of redundant video re-open
- Add error logging to save_fuzzed_frame at every failure point
- Return int status from save_fuzzed_frame for frame count tracking
- Track totalFramesSaved counter through main loop

Coverage improvements:
- Remove dead code: log_memory_info, malloc_with_guard, free_with_guard
  (defined in runner but never called — duplicated in interposer)
- Remove malloc_zone_print from log_gpu_memory_info (caused massive log
  spam: 1377 calls/run, multi-minute ASAN hangs)
- Remove unused includes: malloc/malloc.h, sys/mman.h, duplicate stdio.h
- Remove unused page_align macro
- Lower synthetic frame save interval: every 100th → every 10th iteration
- Reduce log frequency: periodic instead of every-iteration logging

Net: -190 lines dead code, +123 lines working frame save path.
Expected improvement: coverage from 53.65% → ~70%+ lines.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
